senators.name: Todd Young
senators.nerdReason: B.S. in political science from the United States Naval Academy, M.B.A. from University of Chicago, M.A. in American politics from University of London, and J.D. from Indiana University. Four post-secondary degrees from elite institutions. Marine Captain. Co-authored the CHIPS and Science Act promoting semiconductor manufacturing and research. Combines military discipline with academic breadth and genuine policy depth on technology and innovation.
education: J.D., Indiana University McKinney School of Law
M.A. American Politics, University of London
B.S. Political Science, U.S. Naval Academy

senators.name: Jim Banks
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science from Indiana University and M.B.A. from Grace College. Career in real estate, construction, and politics. Navy Reserve Supply Corps officer with deployment to Afghanistan. First-generation college student. Solid credentials but legislative approach is more ideologically driven than analytically rigorous.
experience: U.S. Navy Reserve, Supply Corps Officer

IN-01.name: Frank Mrvan
IN-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from Indiana University Northwest. Career as union steward, township trustee, and state senator. Deep roots in working-class community governance but no analytical, technical, or evidence-based policy background.

IN-02.name: Rudy Yakym
IN-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Notre Dame. Career in business and finance. Won special election in 2022. Business background provides practical skills but no distinctly analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

IN-03.name: Marlin Stutzman
IN-03.notNerdReason: Attended Trine University and Glen Oaks Community College without completing a degree. Career as farmer and state legislator. No academic credentials or analytical policy background.

IN-04.name: Jim Baird
IN-04.nerdReason: B.S. and M.S. in animal science from Purdue University and Ph.D. in animal science (monogastric nutrition) from University of Kentucky. Vietnam War veteran (Bronze Star, two Purple Hearts). Worked as a Purdue University extension agent and animal nutritionist in the feed industry. One of the few members of Congress with a Ph.D. in a scientific field. Brings genuine research expertise to agriculture and science policy.
education: Ph.D. Animal Science (Monogastric Nutrition), University of Kentucky
experience: Vietnam War Veteran (Bronze Star, 2 Purple Hearts)

IN-05.name: Victoria Spartz
IN-05.nerdReason: B.S. in international economics and M.B.A. from National University of Economics in Ukraine. M.P.A. from Indiana University Kelley School of Business. Harvard Business School Executive Education in healthcare. CPA license. Career path from bank teller to Big Four accounting, CFO of Indiana Attorney General's Office, and adjunct faculty at IU Kelley School. Deep quantitative and financial analytical expertise applied to fiscal and healthcare policy.
education: M.P.A., Indiana University Kelley School of Business
B.S. International Economics, National University of Economics (Ukraine)
experience: Adjunct Faculty, IU Kelley School of Business

IN-06.name: Jefferson Shreve
IN-06.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science from Indiana University, M.A. from University of London, and M.B.A. from Purdue University. Three degrees show intellectual breadth. Founded Storage Express self-storage company. Strong business credentials but legislative approach is more entrepreneurial than analytically rigorous or evidence-based.

IN-07.name: Andre Carson
IN-07.notNerdReason: B.A. in criminal justice management from Concordia University and M.S. in business management from Indiana Wesleyan University. Career in law enforcement (Indiana Department of Homeland Security) and city council. Serves on the House Intelligence Committee, requiring analytical engagement with classified information. Solid but approach is more experiential than research-driven.
education: M.S. Business Management, Indiana Wesleyan University
B.A. Criminal Justice Management, Concordia University
experience: House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence

IN-08.name: Mark Messmer
IN-08.notNerdReason: B.S. from Purdue University. Career in business and state legislature. No analytical, technical, or evidence-based policy approach beyond standard legislative work.

IN-09.name: Erin Houchin
IN-09.notNerdReason: B.A. and M.S. in public affairs from Indiana University. Career as policy advisor and state senator. Master's in public affairs shows policy analysis training. Worked as legislative aide and policy advisor. Solid credentials but governance approach driven by conservative principles rather than research methodology.
